evidence_excerpt: After 18 months, she continued to exhibit improved ambulation, decreased dystonia, and enhanced activities of daily living; dystonia scale rating dropped from 3+ to 1+ after intrathecal baclofen pump implantation, indicating improvement with residual symptoms.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Decreased dystonia sustained over 18 months; patient remained on intrathecal baclofen and low-dose clonazepam, indicating ongoing but mild symptoms.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: An intrathecal baclofen pump was implanted, and the dose was titrated up to 1200 mcg per day over 3 months.
Intrathecal baclofen trial and subsequent pump led to decreased tone and dystonia, increased lower extremity range of motion, and improved ambulation.
Dystonia scale rating dropped from 3+ (wheelchair required) to 1+ after pump implantation, with improved ambulation and ADLs.
After 18 months, decreased dystonia was sustained.
After 18 months, she continued to exhibit improved ambulation and enhanced activities of daily living.
She also responded to botulinum toxin type-A injections in her left upper extremity prior to and after pump implantation (post-pump timing not precisely specified).

notes_on_uncertainty: The case report provides 18-month follow-up and emphasizes sustained improvement but does not quantify exact functional independence. Baseline developmental and oromotor deficits may persist and are not fully separable from dystonia-related limitations. Timing of post-pump botulinum injections is not precisely stated.
